scores.correctness_notes: The agent correctly identifies IngressPerAppRequirer as the class to use on the requires side, which the gold standard confirms exists. However, the gold standard focuses on the data schema (application databag with name/model/port, unit databag with host) as the key content from charmlibs documentation, which the agent omits entirely. The agent also uses v2 while gold standard mentions v1 as the recommended library (though v2 is also noted).
scores.hallucination_notes: The agent provides many specific API details not verified by the gold standard: constructor parameters (strip_prefix, redirect_https, scheme), events (on.ready, on.revoked), methods (is_ready()), and property (url). The gold standard explicitly notes that charmlibs docs cover interface specs/data schemas rather than Python library API, so these details come from elsewhere and cannot be verified against the gold standard source. Some may be real but cannot be confirmed; the level of unverified specifics warrants a minor deduction.
